raw_text: Bayesian feedback strengthens belief posteriors when the user confirms the belief was useful.
note: factual default; synthesis describing v1.0 feedback semantics.

raw_text: Independent harmful feedback events accumulate demotion pressure on auto-locked beliefs.
User-locked beliefs are exempt from automatic demotion until the user explicitly unlocks them.
The posterior ranking pass must not reorder locked beliefs relative to one another.
The deferred-feedback sweeper resolves used and harmful events that arrived after the original turn closed.
note: factual default; describes a shipped sweeper.

raw_text: The agent should prefer beliefs with high posterior over beliefs with high prior when ranking is contested.
Promoted beliefs gain a constant prior boost that survives across sessions.
Synthesis rows record the derivation rule and the source belief ids that drove the posterior shift.
Replay-equality on a synthesis row recomputes the same belief id from the same input on every host.
note: factual default; describes determinism contract for synthesis.

raw_text: Synthesis must not introduce new locks; locks come only from explicit user-stated mcp_remember or cli_remember paths.
note: requirement 'must' triggers BELIEF_REQUIREMENT; tests negation 'must not'.
